features.description: A fresh agent can understand project state, startup commands, verification requirements, and next steps without prior chat history.
acceptance: Read `CLAUDE.md` for project architecture and non-negotiable engineering principles.
Read `progress.md` for current state, startup checklist, constraints, and session handoff.
Read `feature_list.json` for machine-readable acceptance criteria and verification status.
Confirm `AGENTS.md` remains a pointer to `CLAUDE.md` and is not expanded during this harness supplement.
features.notes: Verified `progress.md`, `feature_list.json`, and `init.sh` exist; `feature_list.json` parses; `AGENTS.md` was left unchanged as a pointer to `CLAUDE.md`.

features.description: The project has a safe fresh-session bootstrap entry point for dependency setup and baseline verification.
acceptance: Run `./init.sh --skip-build` from the repository root and confirm it checks Bun availability and dependency setup.
Run `./init.sh` from the repository root and confirm it completes the default baseline verification.
Confirm `./init.sh --help` documents safe flags and does not include destructive cleanup or data reset behavior.
features.notes: `./init.sh --help` documented safe flags; default `./init.sh` completed Bun check, `bun install`, `bun run test`, and `bun run build:web` successfully.

features.description: The deterministic offline test suite validates the core prompt/skill evaluation loops without network access.
acceptance: Run `bun run test` from the repository root.
Verify e2e prompt and skill tests pass using mock engine and mock judge paths.
Verify no real LLM endpoint or external network dependency is required for the test run.
features.notes: `bun run test` passed: 48 pass, 0 fail, 160 expect() calls across 8 files.

features.description: The web assets can be built from the TypeScript SPA and Tailwind CSS sources.
acceptance: Run `bun run build:web` from the repository root.
Verify `public/app.css` and `public/main.js` are generated from current sources.
Run this check after UI or design-system changes before claiming UI readiness.
features.notes: `bun run build:web` passed after `bun install`; generated `public/app.css` and `public/main.js`, both ignored by Git.

features.description: Prompt and Skill are modeled as peer evaluation targets with shared experiment infrastructure and different metric taxonomies.
acceptance: Verify `src/core/types.ts` keeps `TargetType` as `prompt | skill`.
Verify prompt metrics include instruction following, output quality, side-effect safety, and reliability.
Verify skill metrics include trigger accuracy, execution reliability, side-effect safety, and composition.
Verify tests cover both prompt and skill target end-to-end loops.
features.notes: Code inspection confirmed peer target and metric definitions; `bun run test` passed prompt and skill e2e suites.

features.description: Failed runs can be attributed through counterfactual replay to a concrete prompt, skill, tool, or base-model fix locus.
acceptance: Run an experiment with failing samples.
Run attribution for the experiment.
Verify attributions include counterfactual evidence, root cause, fix layer, trace step index, and suggested fix.
Verify prompt-instruction, wrong-skill-selection, skill-execution, tool-call, and base-model causes remain distinguishable.
features.notes: `bun run test` passed e2e attribution coverage for prompt and skill loops; source inspection confirmed counterfactual root-cause taxonomy.

features.description: Candidate versions are blocked when pass^k or any category metric regresses against the baseline.
acceptance: Run an A/B prompt or skill experiment with a baseline and candidate version.
Verify pass^k mean, standard deviation, and delta are reported.
Verify the gate fails when any category metric regresses beyond the configured epsilon.
Verify pipeline artifacts include timing, grading, and benchmark JSON outputs.
features.notes: `bun run test` passed gate and e2e pipeline artifact coverage, including degraded candidate gate failure.

features.description: UI work uses the project design language and no-framework DOM helper conventions.
acceptance: Read `DESIGN.md` before UI, layout, visual state, or copy changes.
Use Tailwind CSS v4 tokens from `src/web/app.css` and component patterns from `src/web/components.ts`.
Use `src/web/dom.ts` helper patterns instead of introducing a frontend framework.
Treat prototypes or future design exports as conditional references unless the user explicitly asks to use them.
features.notes: Verified `DESIGN.md` exists and `bun run build:web` passed with the current Tailwind CSS v4 and vanilla TypeScript SPA setup.

conditional_reference_documents.use_when: Only when initializing, repairing, or auditing project harness files.
Only when adapting the reusable harness template to this project.
Mandatory for UI/UX work; otherwise read only when the task touches visual language, layout, components, states, or copy.
